mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals broad economic strength, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2.5%, it signals rising inflation that will compel the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates an overheating labor market, prompting aggressive monetary tightening from the central bank, which is bearish for equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% and 6-month change in CPIAUCSL is positive
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a negative 3-month change in retail sales (RSAFS) is a leading indicator of a consumer-led slowdown, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns during periods where the FEDFUNDS rate has increased over the prior 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) growth points to weakening manufacturing and supply-side activity, acting as a leading indicator for lower corporate profits and negative market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 average 60-day forward returns are non-negative following periods where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects robust consumer confidence and construction sector health, providing a short-term positive impulse to equity market sentiment.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained, above-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms a healthy economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and justifying higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Periods of above-average YoY GDPC1 growth are not followed by statistically significant positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is raising the FEDFUNDS rate while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating, it signals a potential policy error of tightening into a slowdown, which is highly negative for risk assets.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0.25 and QoQ GDPC1 growth is lower than the prior quarter's growth
evidence_refs: fact:1981 recession followed tightening into slowdown
failure_condition: The hit rate for predicting negative 60-day returns is below 55% during triggered periods in a walk-forward test over two full business cycles.
